general: In the Year of the Horse, those born in the Year of the Rooster will experience relatively stable fortunes, as there are no clashes or harmonies. Furthermore, with the arrival of a series of powerful auspicious stars, they will encounter helpful people, and their romantic luck and interpersonal relationships will be exceptionally strong. They will reap rewards in all aspects, including wealth, career, and love, making it a truly favorable year. During the Year of the Horse, those born in the Year of the Rooster will be blessed by auspicious stars such as "Red Phoenix," "Taiyin," and "Tianyi," which will ensure smooth and successful interpersonal relationships. "Red Phoenix" represents romantic luck; unmarried individuals will have many opportunities to meet their ideal partner and begin a long-term relationship, so they must actively seize the opportunity. For couples, if their relationship is stable, the Year of the Horse is also a good time to consider marriage and embark on a new chapter in their lives. However, for married individuals, in a year with strong romantic luck, they should resist external temptations to avoid getting caught up in a love triangle due to wrong choices. "Red Phoenix" not only enhances relationships but also boosts the personal charm of those born in the Year of the Rooster, making them more likable both externally and internally, and is expected to attract the attention of helpful people. Regarding "Tianyi," this is a powerful benefactor star. In the new year, elders, superiors, subordinates, and even family and friends will provide ample support and care to those born in the Year of the Rooster, enabling smooth progress in their careers. "Taiyin" symbolizes female benefactors. If your direct supervisor at work is female, you may have a promotion opportunity in the Year of the Horse. If you are in business and female customers dominate, such as in the women's clothing, beauty, cosmetics, and jewelry industries, your sales will improve, and your income is expected to grow steadily. However, the Year of the Horse also brings the inauspicious stars "Guansuo" and "Goushen." These astrological signs indicate potential entanglements in romantic relationships or financial disputes. Therefore, you need to be extra cautious when dealing with emotional or financial matters, keeping accounts clear to avoid losing friends over money. In addition, the influence of "Hongsi" may lead to "spending money on joyous occasions" due to wedding preparations, while the interference of "Guansuo" and "Goushen" may also hinder romantic luck. It is advisable to avoid joint investments or excessive financial transactions with newly acquainted members of the opposite sex to avoid unnecessary misunderstandings and disputes. Overall, the auspicious stars in the Year of the Horse bring relatively harmonious interpersonal relationships. Business owners can leverage their networks to expand business opportunities, while employees may receive more attention from their superiors. Investments may yield returns through information provided by respected elders. However, despite the strong support of the Year of the Horse, the influence of inauspicious stars should not be underestimated. It is necessary to guard against emotional troubles or financial disputes, and all matters should be carefully considered to avoid unexpected situations that could damage the overall fortune.
